purpose: Environment-specific guidance for locating JavaScript/web exercise task units and determining NOT_STARTED vs HAS_ATTEMPTED across file-based Codio projects.

primary_boundaries: explicit editable markers such as // YOUR CODE STARTS HERE / // YOUR CODE ENDS HERE
TODO blocks or numbered TODO comments
clearly incomplete functions, event listeners, handlers, or initialization steps
task_detection.file_selection_rule: Do not assume the student always edits exercise_files/scripts/interactivity.js. Use assessment-opened files, editable files, and guide instructions to determine the relevant student work files.
task_detection.freeze_rule: Code inside FREEZE / PROVIDED regions is not student work unless the instructions clearly indicate that the student should modify it.
task_detection.nested_todo_rule: If multiple TODO comments appear inside one function and are tightly coupled, treat the function region as one task with substeps. If the TODOs are independent, split them into separate tasks.
task_detection.no_marker_fallback: If explicit markers are missing, infer task regions from the opened/editable files, guide instructions, and instructor-reference structure.

task_families.name: conditionals_and_boolean_logic
examples: branching behavior based on form inputs

task_families.name: arrays_loops_and_iteration
examples: construct an array of image paths
iterate over an array to generate DOM elements
compute next/previous item in an array

task_families.name: dom_selection_and_update
examples: set attributes like src, alt, class

task_families.name: event_handling_and_user_interaction
examples: change handler for a select element

task_families.name: timer_state_and_animation
examples: hide or reveal items over time

task_families.name: async_fetch_and_redraw
examples: clear and redraw UI from returned data

explicit_markers: // YOUR CODE STARTS HERE ... // YOUR CODE ENDS HERE
work_zone_rules.fallback_rule: If explicit markers are missing, infer the student's work zone from the incomplete region in the editable file, guide instructions, and the instructor reference.
work_zone_rules.student_work_outside_zone: If the student writes real solution code outside the expected region but it is clearly meant to solve the task, count it as HAS_ATTEMPTED and note that in notes.

has_attempted: an if statement or boolean condition
a loop or partial loop structure
a DOM query or DOM update
a function body with real logic
a fetch call or promise chain
a one-line implementation such as setting text, attaching a listener, or returning a computed value
not_started: debugging output only, with no task logic
special_cases: Partial code that would not run yet still counts as HAS_ATTEMPTED if it is clearly real implementation.
Comment-only edits do not count as an attempt.
One meaningful executable line is enough to count as HAS_ATTEMPTED.

examples.label: event_listener_attempt
examples.why: A partial event-listener implementation is a real attempt.

examples.label: comment_only_not_started
examples.why: Comment-only edits do not count as implementation.

examples.label: conditional_attempt
examples.why: A real conditional expression is enough to show the student has started.

examples.label: dom_update_attempt
examples.why: A one-line DOM update is real task logic.

examples.label: array_iteration_attempt
examples.why: A loop to generate or process items is real implementation.

examples.label: function_stub_not_started
examples.why: An empty function body without logic is not started.

examples.label: function_attempt
examples.why: Even a wrong return statement is still implementation.

examples.label: timer_attempt
examples.why: Starting the timer logic counts as a real attempt.

examples.label: fetch_attempt
examples.why: A fetch and parse chain is meaningful implementation.

course_pattern_notes: CIS561 includes introductory DOM/event-listener and text-content update tasks.
CIS562 includes conditionals, boolean logic, and form-driven branching tasks.
CIS563 includes arrays, loops, DOM creation, and gallery interaction tasks.
CIS564 includes helper functions, localStorage, scope, and next/previous navigation tasks.
CIS565 includes D3 rendering, timer-based animation, and async fetch/redraw tasks.

output_mapping_hints.location_guidance: Use file path plus line hints when reasonably inferable. If exact lines are unclear, approximate the task region around the relevant TODO or incomplete function.
